title: Chicken with Goat Cheese & Basil
description: From Barefoot Contessa At Home — Ina's go-to for her Dinner in a Hurry menu. Chicken breasts stuffed with creamy goat cheese and fresh basil, wrapped in prosciutto, and roasted until golden. It looks incredibly impressive but takes only 10 minutes to assemble. The prosciutto crisps up and bastes the chicken as it roasts.
instructions: Place each chicken breast between 2 sheets of parchment paper or plastic wrap and pound to ¼-inch thickness with a meat mallet.
Spread 1 ounce of goat cheese on each chicken breast. Place 4 basil leaves on top of the goat cheese. Roll up each breast and wrap with 2 slices of prosciutto, covering the chicken completely.
Place the chicken rolls seam-side down in a baking dish. Brush with olive oil and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Roast for 30 to 35 minutes, until the prosciutto is golden brown and crispy and the chicken is cooked through (165°F). Allow to rest for 5 minutes before serving.
tips: Secure with a toothpick if needed to keep the rolls from unraveling during roasting.
The prosciutto bastes the chicken as it roasts, keeping it incredibly moist.
This dish can be assembled up to 24 hours ahead and refrigerated — just bring to room temperature before roasting.
Serve with roasted cherry tomatoes and sautéed corn for Ina's complete Dinner in a Hurry menu.
Source: Barefoot Contessa At Home by Ina Garten (2006)
